name: Switch
purpose: Toggles the state of a single setting on or off.
whenToUse: To toggle a single setting or feature on or off.
When changes should take effect immediately without requiring a save button.
whenNotToUse: For selecting multiple options from a list (use Checkbox).
For selecting a single option from a mutually exclusive list (use Radio).
When explicit submission or confirmation is required before applying the change.

accessibility.touchTargets: Ensure minimum touch target of 48x48dp, usually achieved by wrapping the Switch in a SwitchListTile or adding padding.
accessibility.semantics: Always associate the switch with a descriptive label using SwitchListTile or Semantics so screen readers can announce its purpose.
accessibility.stateFeedback: Screen readers automatically announce the toggled state based on the value property.

flutterMappingNotes: Map the 'on' or 'off' state to the `value` property (true = on, false = off).
Use the `onChanged` callback to handle state updates and trigger rebuilds.
The switch automatically enters the 'disabled' state when `onChanged` is set to null.
Map track.on.background and track.off.background using `trackColor` (MaterialStateProperty) or the simpler `activeTrackColor` and `inactiveTrackColor`.
Map thumb.on.background and thumb.off.background using `thumbColor` (MaterialStateProperty) or the simpler `activeThumbColor` and `inactiveThumbColor`.
Use `trackOutlineColor` and `trackOutlineWidth` (MaterialStateProperty) to customize or remove the track's border.
Use `thumbIcon` (MaterialStateProperty) to add icons to the thumb for different states (e.g., checkmark when on, cross when off).
Use `overlayColor` (MaterialStateProperty) to define visual feedback for pressed and focus states.
